doc_title: BEYOND TRADITIONAL MAGNETIC RESONANCE PROCESSING WITH ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE *

items.evidence_span: In our recent publication, we introduced a DNN architecture called WNN, specifically designed to grasp 1D patterns over the entire NMR spectrum in the frequency domain, such as specific patterns of NUS aliasing artifacts and peak multiples in homo-decoupling [8].
Compressed Sensing Iterative Soft Thresholding algorithm (CS-IST) [24, 25],
Compressed Sensing Iterative Soft Thresholding algorithm (CS-IST) [24, 25],
using a more extended and advanced set of the NUScon metrics [8, 26].
Traditionally, this problem is solved by the brute force approach of repeated measures or postexperiment resampling of the data [26, 28].
DNN offers a much more efficient alternative [29].
It is possible to train a network to predict the quality of the results generated by any method [30] by employing the negative log-likelihood (NLL) as the loss function during the training stage.
